explanation:
The remark for claim_3_6 (SplitTopologicalOrder) assigns new indices only to the split copies in $W$: "for a node $v_j \in W$ with index $j$ the node $v_j^0$ the index $j-\frac{1}{3}$ and $v_j^1$ the index $j+\frac{1}{3}$, and then ordering all nodes according to their index value." But the node set of $G_{\spl(W)}$ (per def_3_11) is $J \cup (V \setminus W) \cup W^0 \cup W^1$, and the remark says nothing about the indices of the unsplit nodes $v_j \in J \cup (V \setminus W)$. The only interpretation that makes the procedure well-defined is that each unsplit $v_j$ keeps its original index $j$ from the enumeration $v_1 < v_2 < \cdots < v_n$ on $J \cup V$.

A pedantic reader applying the literal procedure would find unsplit nodes have no assigned index and would be unable to perform the final "ordering all nodes according to their index value" step. The natural fix is an explicit clause such as "and to each $v_j \in (J \cup V) \setminus W$ the index $j$." Downstream formalisation must supply this retention rule (in the Lean encoding the issue does not arise because we define the strict-total-order directly on $\mathrm{SplitNode}\;\mathrm{Node}$ via a lexicographic-style comparison on (underlying-node, tag-in-\{-1,0,+1\}) and never refer to integer indices; but tex-faithful rewordings must spell it out).

Corner case making the gap concrete: with $n = 2$ and $W = \{v_2\}$, the remark explicitly assigns $v_2^0 \mapsto 5/3$ and $v_2^1 \mapsto 7/3$. The literal text gives no index for $v_1$, so the relative order between $v_1$ and the split copies is unspecified by what is written; only the implicit "$v_1$ keeps index $1$" recovers the correct order $v_1 < v_2^0 < v_2^1$.

This subtlety was surfaced by the working-phase LN wording-check on def_3_15 (Colliders and non-colliders). A directed self-loop $(v,v) \in E$ is permitted by the CDMG definition (def_3_1: $E \subseteq (J \cup V) \times V$ has no $v \ne v$ exclusion -- only the loose-edge set $L$ explicitly forbids self-loops). Per the edge-mark notation in def_3_2, the macros decode to $v_1 \tuh v_2 \iff (v_1, v_2) \in E$ and $v_1 \hut v_2 \iff (v_2, v_1) \in E$, so for a self-loop $(v,v) \in E$ both $v \tuh v$ and $v \hut v$ hold simultaneously.

The literal classification in def_3_15 then breaks mutual exclusivity. For a walk position $k$ with $a_{k-1} = (v,v)$ a directed self-loop (so $v_{k-1} = v_k = v$) and $a_k$ a directed edge into $v_k$ from $v_{k+1}$ (so $v_k \hut v_{k+1}$, and thus $v_k \hus v_{k+1}$):

- "left chain" pattern $v_{k-1} \hut v_k \hus v_{k+1}$: matches (the self-loop satisfies $v_{k-1} \hut v_k$ vacuously, and the right edge satisfies $v_k \hus v_{k+1}$) -> classified as **non-collider**.
- "collider" pattern $v_{k-1} \suh v_k \hus v_{k+1}$: also matches (the self-loop also satisfies $v_{k-1} \tuh v_k$, hence $v_{k-1} \suh v_k$, and the right edge satisfies $v_k \hus v_{k+1}$) -> classified as **collider**.

A single position is therefore literally classified as **both** a non-collider and a collider, breaking the "if it falls into one of the following cases" / "is of the form" wording that the LN relies on for mutual exclusivity. The mark-count gloss is similarly ambiguous: the self-loop has its arrowhead and tail both anchored at $v_k$, and the tex does not specify whether that contributes 0, 1, or 2 to the count.

Downstream impact. Self-cycles are actively handled in this codebase (Refactor_marginalize_loose_self_cycle, completed 2026-06-13). Every concept that consumes "collider on a walk" / "non-collider on a walk" inherits this ambiguity for walks that traverse a self-loop: def_3_16 (blockable / unblockable non-colliders), def_3_17 ($\sigma$-blocked walks), claim_3_25 (i$\sigma$-separation), claim_3_27 (label-Roman characterisation). Any encoding that uses unordered set-membership in $E$ -- as opposed to the walk's *traversed-edge orientation* -- will hit this issue.

Expected resolution direction. The walk encoding in Section3_1/Walks.lean carries an explicit traversal direction per edge (each $a_k$ in the walk is an ordered pair, even when the underlying undirected edge could be read either way). The natural Lean encoding of def_3_15 will read the mark at $v_k$ on each of the two walk-incident edges $a_{k-1}, a_k$ using *the walk's own ordering*, not an arbitrary $E$-membership check. Concretely: for $a_{k-1} = (u,w)$ in the walk's directed traversal, the mark at $v_k$ (right side) is "arrowhead" iff $w = v_k$ and $(u,w) \in E$, and the mark at $v_{k-1}$ (left side) is "arrowhead" iff $u = v_{k-1}$ and $(w,u) \in E$ -- so for the self-loop $(v,v)$ traversed forward, only the head-side mark is read. This breaks the ambiguity by *deciding* which traversal direction the walk has committed to. Every downstream consumer will read marks via the same walk-traversal-aware predicate, so the convention propagates uniformly.

This subtlety is recorded here so that (a) the formalize-in-tex / formalize-in-lean workers for def_3_15 know to pin a tie-breaking convention rather than translate the literal "$\hut$/$\tuh$" relations into unordered $E$-checks, and (b) any future row that surfaces a "collider on this walk" result against a self-loop traversal can audit whether the convention was followed consistently.

In `lem:replace_walk` (claim_3_27 in chapter 3), the hypothesis "Suppose $v_i \in \Sc^G(v_j)$ for some $i,j \in \{0,\dots,n\}$ with $i < j$" requires only that the *positions* differ, not the *vertices* — so $v_i$ and $v_j$ can be the same vertex when $\pi$ revisits a node. In that case the prescribed replacement "shortest directed path $v_i \tuh \cdots \tuh v_j$" (case (i)) or "$v_i \hut \cdots \hut v_j$" (case (ii)) collapses to the trivial path of length $0$ — just the single node $v_i$ — since a CDMG has no self-loops. The literal LN notation "$v_i \tuh \cdots \tuh v_j$" with "$\cdots$" sandwiched between two $\tuh$-symbols *looks* like it requires at least one directed edge, so the length-0 reading is not visibly contained in the formula. The proof body does acknowledge the case ("If $v_i=v_j$ then also $v_i$ is $C$-$\sigma$-open on $\pi'$" and the later "Note that this holds also when $v_i=v_j$"), but the lemma statement leaves it implicit. For the Lean formalization this matters: the encoding admits length-0 directed walks (`Walk.nil v hv` satisfies `Walk.IsDirectedWalk` definitionally per `Section3_1/Walks.lean:942`), so the "shortest directed walk from $v$ to $v$" is `Walk.nil v hv` and no degenerate-branch carve-out is required in the formal statement. Downstream rows that mention "shortest directed walk" should similarly treat the trivial path as admitted. An `addition_to_the_LN`-style clause in the rewritten canonical tex for claim_3_27 will record this clarification at the spec level too.

In `lem:replace_walk` (claim_3_27 in chapter 3), Case (ii) sub-case 2 of the proof writes "So there must be a smallest number $k \in \{i,\dots,j\}$ such that a collider appears at $v_k$ on $\pi$" and then draws the picture "$\pi:\qquad \cdots v_{i-1} \suh v_i \tuh \cdots \tuh v_k \hus \cdots \sus v_j \hus v_{j+1} \cdots$". The diagram literally forces $\tuh$-edges between $v_i$ and $v_k$, which only makes sense when $k > i$. But the definition "smallest $k \in \{i, \dots, j\}$" admits $k = i$ — precisely the case where $v_i$ itself is the first collider (i.e., the original subwalk has $v_i \hus v_{i+1}$ on $\pi$). In that case the picture has no $\tuh$-edges to the left of $v_k$ at all, and the supporting ancestry argument "Since $v_i \in \Anc^G(v_k)$ (otherwise $v_k$ would not be the first collider appearing after $v_i$)" reads awkwardly because there is no "after $v_i$" — the collider is *at* $v_i$. The conclusion $v_i \in \Anc^G(C)$ still holds trivially (via $v_i = v_k \in \Anc^G(C)$ by $\pi$-openness using `Anc` reflexivity), so the lemma itself is fine. But the proof's diagram + ancestry justification literally cover only the strict $k > i$ case; the $k = i$ case is silently relegated to "the reader will see it works trivially". For the Lean formalization in Manager B's pass, this matters: the proof body in `<ref>_proof_<title>.tex` will need to either (a) explicitly split on $k = i$ vs.\ $k > i$ before invoking the ancestry chain, or (b) phrase the ancestry argument so that the reflexive case $v_i = v_k$ falls out naturally (e.g., "$v_i \in \Anc^G(v_k)$ because the subwalk $v_i \tuh \cdots \tuh v_k$ is a directed path, possibly of length 0"). Option (b) is cleaner if `Anc` is defined reflexively (which it is — `FamilyRelationships.lean`: $v \in \Anc^G(v)$). This subtlety also dovetails with `vi_equals_vj_trivial_directed_path`: both are length-0 directed-path edge cases that the LN's pictorial notation hides but the Lean encoding must handle as the trivial `Walk.nil` case.

The lemma `lem:replace_walk` (= claim_3_27) is FALSE under the literal LN statement combined with the canonical Lean encoding of self-loops as forwardE WalkSteps satisfying `IsInto` on both endpoints (per def_3_15 and the chapter-wide subtlety `self_loop_makes_tuh_and_hut_simultaneously_true`).

Specifically, case (i)'s trigger — "(i) a shortest directed path $v_i \tuh \cdots \tuh v_j$ in $G$ if $j = n$ or if $v_j \tuh v_{j+1}$ on $\pi$" — fires for the edge case where $v_{j+1} = v_j$ and $a_j$ is a directed self-loop $(v_j, v_j) \in E$. In that case, the prescribed replacement (shortest directed path $v_i \tuh \cdots \tuh v_j$) can introduce a collider at $v_j$ on $\pi'$ that wasn't present on $\pi$, breaking $C$-$\sigma$-openness.

CONCRETE COUNTER-EXAMPLE (sound under the canonical encoding, verified by the `verify_tex_proof` worker, session id `0b28bf97-9527-4042-b605-3bca225ff00f`). Let $G = (J = \emptyset, V = \{a, b\}, E = \{(a,b), (b,a), (b,b)\}, L = \emptyset)$. Then $\Sc^G(b) = \{a, b\}$ (the directed cycle $a \to b \to a$ makes $\{a, b\}$ a single SCC). Let $\pi = (a, e_0, b, e_1, b)$ where $e_0 = \texttt{backwardE}\{u := a, v := b\}$ storing the directed edge $(b, a) \in E$, and $e_1 = \texttt{forwardE}\{u := b, v := b\}$ storing the directed self-loop $(b, b) \in E$. Take $C = \emptyset$.

VERIFY $\pi$ is $C$-$\sigma$-open. At position 1 ($= b$), the incoming step $e_0$ contributes IsInto $b$ via $w = u$, but $u = a \neq b$, so $+0$; the outgoing step $e_1$ is a forwardE self-loop and its IsInto fires on $w = v = b$, so $+1$. Total arrowhead count at position 1 is $1$, so position 1 is a non-collider on $\pi$. The non-collider is unblockable: its outgoing walk-edges connect to $a$ and $b$, both in $\Sc^G(b) = \{a, b\}$. Endpoints $a$ and $b$ are not in $C = \emptyset$. So $\pi$ is $C$-$\sigma$-open.

APPLY claim_3_27 with $i = 0, j = 1$. Hypothesis: $v_0 = a \in \Sc^G(b) = \Sc^G(v_1)$ ✓; $i = 0 < j = 1$ ✓. Case (i) trigger fires since $a_1 = e_1 = \texttt{forwardE}$ storing $(b, b) \in E$ with first component $v_j = b$ (this satisfies "$v_j \tuh v_{j+1}$ on $\pi$" under the encoding). Replacement: shortest directed path $v_0 \tuh \cdots \tuh v_1$, i.e. shortest directed path $a \to b$ in $G$ is just $(a, \texttt{forwardE}(a, b), b)$. So $\pi' = (a, \texttt{forwardE}(a, b), b, \texttt{forwardE}(b, b), b)$.

OBSERVE $\pi'$ is $C$-$\sigma$-BLOCKED. At position 1 ($= b$) on $\pi'$, the incoming step $\texttt{forwardE}\{u := a, v := b\}$ contributes IsInto $b$ via $w = v = b$ ($+1$); the outgoing step $\texttt{forwardE}\{u := b, v := b\}$ (self-loop) contributes IsInto $b$ via $w = v = b$ ($+1$). Total arrowhead count $2$, so position 1 is a COLLIDER on $\pi'$. $C$-$\sigma$-open at a collider requires $v_1 = b \in \Anc^G(C) = \Anc^G(\emptyset) = \emptyset$, which fails. So $\pi'$ is $\sigma$-blocked at position 1, contradicting the lemma's conclusion.

REMEDIATION (verifier-recommended, confirmed against the counter-example). Tighten case (i)'s trigger to "$j = n$, or ($v_j \tuh v_{j+1}$ on $\pi$ AND $v_{j+1} \neq v_j$)", so self-loops at step $j$ fall into case (ii). In the counter-example above, case (ii) prescribes the shortest reverse-direction path $v_i \hut \cdots \hut v_j$, i.e. the shortest directed path $b \to a$ which is the single edge $(b, a)$; the replacement subwalk is then $(a, \texttt{backwardE}\{u := a, v := b\}\text{ storing } (b, a), b)$, which is byte-identical to the original subwalk $e_0$, hence $\pi' = \pi$ and is trivially $\sigma$-open. The tightening should be folded into `addition_to_the_LN` on the claim_3_27 row (operator action), with cascading updates to `claim_3_27_statement_LabelRoman.tex` and `claim_3_27_proof_LabelRoman.tex`.

RELATED, DISTINCT, PREVIOUSLY-SURFACED PHENOMENA. (a) `vi_equals_vj_trivial_directed_path` (initialization-phase) — length-0 directed path when $v_i = v_j$ as vertices; different edge case, applies even without self-loops. (b) `case_ii_k_equals_i_diagram_inconsistency` (initialization-phase) — separate diagram-vs-formal-text issue in case (ii)'s smallest-collider sub-case. (c) `self_loop_makes_tuh_and_hut_simultaneously_true` (registered against def_3_15) — captures why the IsInto encoding treats self-loops as contributing arrowheads on both ends, but does NOT flag that this propagates into a flaw in claim_3_27's case structure. The current entry sits in the same family as (c) but is one cascade hop downstream — it's the consumer-side manifestation of the encoding choice.

IMPLICATIONS FOR THE ROW'S REMEDIATION CHOICE. claim_3_27 cannot be proven as literally stated under the canonical encoding. Three options exist for the next turn: (1) tighten case (i)'s trigger via `addition_to_the_LN` + canonical statement update + proof update (most aligned with LN intent; preserves the lemma as useful infrastructure for downstream `claim_3_23` / `claim_3_24` / `claim_3_25`, which the reordering rationale flagged as consumers); (2) emit `mistake` and prove $\neg$(literal LN claim) via the counter-example above (would record `proven="disproven"`, leaving downstream consumers without a usable replacement lemma); (3) `refactor` def_3_15 / `WalkStep.IsInto` so self-loops contribute exactly one arrowhead (heavy — cascades through every downstream consumer of `IsInto` and would invalidate the existing subtlety `self_loop_makes_tuh_and_hut_simultaneously_true`).
